Applies conservation of momentum in one direction to calculate v Or Applies conservation of momentum in one direction to calculate momentum of child B Use of E k = ½ mv Or E k = 푝푝 2푚푚 Initial E k = 130 J and Final E k = 110 J (allow ecf from (a)) Initial E k is not equal to final E k , so collision not elastic Or Comparison of initial E k and final E k with correct conclusion for their calculated values (1) (1) (1) (1) Example of calculation 푣푣= 1.36 ms −1 ×45 kgsin ( ) 30 kg sin(30.9) = 2.16ms −1 퐸퐸 k = 45 kg× � 2.38 m s −1 � = 127 J (A before) 퐸퐸 k = 45 kg× � 1.36 m s −1 � = 41.6 J (A after) 퐸퐸 k = 30 kg× � 2.16 m s −1 � = 70.0 J (B after) 41.6 J + 70.0 J = 112 J 127 J > 112 J, so it is inelastic Total for question 17 8 Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark

The charged particle/pion experiences a force in a magnetic field. The force is perpendicular to the motion of the π + This provides a centripetal force/acceleration, so the particle moves in a circle (1) (1) (1) Allow spiral/curve for circle The second particle must be neutral to conserve charge Or the second particle must be neutral as it does not (ionise and) leave tracks The second particle must be a lepton to conserve lepton number (1) (1) Allow the second particle has a lepton number of +1 to conserve lepton number Allow 1 mark for “the second particle is neutral and a lepton” 18 (b) (ii) (The second particle must go in the opposite direction) to conserve momentum (1) Total for question 18 6 Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark *19(a) This question assesses a student’s ability to show a coherent and logically structured answer with linkages and fully-sustained reasoning. Marks are awarded for indicative content and for how the answer is structured and shows lines of reasoning. The following table shows how the marks should be awarded for indicative content. Indicative content IC1 Almost all the alpha particles are undeflected IC2 This shows the atom is mainly empty space IC3 Some alpha particles are scattered through small angles IC4 This shows there is a concentration of charge IC5 A few alpha particles are scattered through angles greater than 90° IC6 This shows (almost all) the mass of an atom is concentrated in a very small space IC points IC mark Max linkage mark Max final mark 6 4 2 6 5 3 2 5 4 3 1 4 3 2 1 3 2 2 0 2 1 1 0 1 0 0 0 0 The following table shows how the marks should be awarded for structure and lines of reasoning. EITHER As the rod/conductor moves through the field, there is a change of flux (linkage) This induces an e.m.f. (across the ends of the rod) The e.m.f. acts to oppose the change that caused it Or Direction of e.m.f. given by Lenz’s law Induced e.m.f. acts to oppose battery e.m.f. Or Induced e.m.f. reduces current in rod (1) (1) (1) (1) Allow As the rod/conductor moves through the field, it cuts lines of magnetic flux Or As the rod/conductor moves through the field, it cuts magnetic field lines in place of change of flux (linkage) When induced e.m.f. is equal to battery e.m.f., resultant force is zero (so acceleration is zero so constant velocity is reached) Or When the current is zero resultant force is zero (so acceleration is zero (so constant velocity is reached) OR As the rod/conductor moves through the field, there is a change of flux (linkage) This induces an e.m.f. (across the ends of the rod) This produces a (induced) current in a complete circuit The (induced) current produces its own magnetic field and a force in the opposite direction When the two opposing forces are equal, resultant force is zero (so acceleration is zero so constant velocity is reached) (1) (1) (1) (1) (1) (1) Allow cuts lines of magnetic flux Or cuts magnetic field lines in place of change of flux (linkage) Total for question 21
